site_analysis_review.platform_notes: Site analysis labels platform as 'custom'. This is partially correct — Calvin Klein UK uses a headless React/Next.js frontend backed by Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC). The frontend is fully custom JS-rendered, explaining why all common selectors return NOT FOUND. PVH Corp (parent company) builds these custom storefronts.
site_analysis_review.anti_bot_notes: Confirmed: direct HTTP times out, standard browser blocked, UC Chrome succeeds. Fingerprint-level detection (likely Cloudflare or similar WAF) at medium severity.
site_analysis_review.connectivity_notes: uc_chrome_none confirmed by probe. JS rendering required — page is 880K chars after rendering. Residential proxy NOT needed.
site_analysis_review.jsonld_notes: Confirmed: Product JSON-LD exists with name, sku, description, brand, image, color, mpn. Offers are EMPTY — price not in JSON-LD. BreadcrumbList JSON-LD also exists.

corrections.correction: GBP — this is calvinklein.CO.UK, prices should be in British Pounds Sterling
corrections.evidence: TLD is .co.uk, site serves UK market

corrections.correction: custom headless React/Next.js + Salesforce Commerce Cloud backend
corrections.evidence: PVH Corp pattern. URL pattern includes SFCC-style product ID (LV047F832GXBS). JSON-LD follows schema.org standard.

connectivity.notes: Direct HTTP times out. Standard Playwright browser blocked by fingerprint detection. UC Chrome (undetected browser) bypasses protection without proxy. Page is fully JS-rendered (880K chars). Use seleniumbase_uc scraping mechanism.

extraction_methods.hybrid_details: Use JSON-LD for title, sku, description, brand, image, color, mpn. Use DOM extraction via JS evaluation for price, availability, original_price, and variants. All DOM extraction requires UC Chrome.
extraction_methods.structured_data_notes: Product JSON-LD has most fields but offers is empty. BreadcrumbList JSON-LD provides category hierarchy.

fields_missing.offers: EMPTY — no price, availability, or currency data

jsonld_extraction.extraction_code: document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]').forEach(el => { const data = JSON.parse(el.textContent); if (data['@type'] === 'Product') { /* extract fields */ } })

url.notes: Direct URL from browser. Normalize double-slash to single-slash if present.

status_code.notes: Set by scraper from HTTP response. Note: UC Chrome may return 0 as HTTP status since it's browser-based.

scraped_at.notes: ISO-8601 timestamp set by scraper at extraction time.

examples: Price extracted from DOM, not JSON-LD
remarks.notes: Set by scraper. Should note that price requires DOM extraction (not JSON-LD).

title.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.name
title.notes: Available in JSON-LD. Page title also contains: 'Scoop Neck Ribbed Tank Top Calvin Klein® | LV047F832GXBS' — can regex extract from title as fallback: document.title.split('Calvin Klein')[0].trim()

price.selector: DOM — requires JS rendering to find price element
price.notes: JSON-LD offers is EMPTY — price must come from rendered DOM. The page uses custom CSS modules so exact selectors unknown until browser available. The JS extraction uses pattern-based search as fallback. GB pounds (£) expected for .co.uk site.

original_price.js_extraction:
// Look for strikethrough/compare-at price elements
[...document.querySelectorAll('[class*="compare"], [class*="was"], [class*="original"], [class*="strike"], s, del')].map(el => el.textContent.trim()).filter(t => /£\d+/.test(t))
original_price.notes: May not exist if product is not on sale. Look for strikethrough styling, 'was' pricing, or compare-at elements.

currency.notes: Inferred from .co.uk TLD. Could also extract from price prefix (£). Site analysis incorrectly stated EUR.

availability.notes: Not available in JSON-LD offers. Must extract from rendered DOM text. Could also check 'Add to Bag' button state — disabled = out of stock.

sku.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.sku
sku.notes: Also extractable from URL slug (last segment after final hyphen). Also in page title after the | separator.

brand.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.brand?.name

description.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.description
description.notes: Available in JSON-LD. May be long — consider truncating to reasonable length.

image.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.image
image.notes: JSON-LD image field typically contains the main product image URL. May be a string or array.

color.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.color

mpn.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelector('script[type="application/ld+json"]').textContent); const product = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product') : ld; product?.mpn
mpn.notes: Manufacturer Part Number available in JSON-LD.

category.notes: Available in second JSON-LD block (BreadcrumbList). Provides full category path.

location.notes: Inferred from .co.uk domain. Calvin Klein UK ships from UK warehouse.

rating.notes: Not in JSON-LD. May or may not exist on page. Probe's rating selector was NOT FOUND.

review_count.notes: Not in JSON-LD. May not exist on all products.

variants.analysis: The product page likely has color and size variants given the product type (tank top). Variant data is typically in the rendered DOM since the page is 880K chars. However, exact variant selectors cannot be determined without browser access to inspect DOM.
variants.price_changes_per_variant: Unknown — need browser access to verify
variants.availability_changes_per_variant: Unknown — need browser access to verify

page_structure.title_tag: Scoop Neck Ribbed Tank Top Calvin Klein® | LV047F832GXBS
page_structure.sku_from_url: URL last segment after final hyphen: LV047F832GXBS
notes: Page is fully JS-rendered — no meaningful content in static HTML
CSS classes are hashed/minified — no human-readable class names
JSON-LD provides 7 product fields but NOT price
Price, availability, and variants must be extracted from rendered DOM
UC Chrome (undetected browser) required for access

mechanism_reassessment.notes: Agree with site_analyzer's stealth_browser recommendation. SeleniumBase UC Chrome is the correct implementation of stealth_browser strategy. The page requires JS rendering AND anti-bot bypass. Direct HTTP is impossible (timeouts). Standard Playwright is blocked. Only UC Chrome succeeds.
mechanism_reassessment.extraction_strategy:
1. Launch UC Chrome, navigate to product URL
2. Wait for full page load (880K chars expected)
3. Extract JSON-LD for: title, sku, description, brand, image, color, mpn, category
4. Execute JS to find price elements by text pattern (£ followed by digits)
5. Execute JS to find availability status
6. Execute JS to find variants if present
7. Wait 3-5 seconds between page loads

confidence_notes: Medium confidence due to browser unavailability. JSON-LD fields are well-mapped and confirmed by probe. Price, availability, and variant selectors use pattern-based fallback extraction since exact CSS selectors could not be tested against the live DOM. The extraction strategy is robust (multiple fallback patterns) but selectors should be refined once browser access is available for testing.
